story.text: As the loop staffing its own gates and resuming its own idle sessions, I need the stdin=DEVNULL guard that keeps a headless model child from hanging on inherited stdin to be STRUCTURAL — one shared helper every spawner calls — so a second spawner cannot silently re-introduce the 600s-hang the gate fixed once (done-line 0168). The probe pen's detached `claude --resume` re-introduced exactly that bug (issue #411), because the guard lived at one call site as a lone correct call plus a prose comment.

briefing.headline: One headless-spawn helper so the stdin=DEVNULL guard cannot be forgotten
briefing.value: A headless `claude -p` / `claude --resume … -p …` that inherits the parent's live stdin HANGS (the 600s timeouts #390/#391/#393/#396, fixed once in gate.py with stdin=DEVNULL, done-line 0168). That guard lived at ONE call site, so continue-probe/probe.py's detached `_spawn` re-introduced the exact bug — it closed stdout/stderr but left stdin inherited. This moves the guard into one shared helper (.claude/skills/_spawn/headless.py) that ALWAYS sets stdin=subprocess.DEVNULL with no override; both spawners now call it, which de-duplicates AND fixes the probe hang.
briefing.why_now: The fleet only staffs its own gates and resumes its own idle sessions if those headless spawns return instead of hanging. A second spawner already regressed the fix silently; without a structural guard a third will too.
briefing.if_accepted: There is one place a headless child is born and it cannot be born with an open stdin; gate.py keeps its captured-output behavior and probe.py keeps its detached fire-and-forget, both through the helper; a §10 test asserts stdin==DEVNULL across both capture modes and both detached modes.
briefing.if_rejected: The guard stays a lone call plus a comment; the probe pen keeps hanging on inherited stdin, and the next spawner copies the bug, not the fix.
briefing.cost_of_wrong_call: Low and reversible: a small stdlib helper plus two call-site rewires that preserve each call's existing behavior; reverting is deleting one module and restoring two calls. No truth moved, no new write path, no model actually spawned by the change or its test.
briefing.mechanism: headless_spawn(argv, *, cwd, timeout=None, detached=False, capture=True, runner=subprocess.run) always sets stdin=subprocess.DEVNULL; capture=True -> stdout/stderr=PIPE (text-decoded), capture=False -> DEVNULL; detached=False -> subprocess.run(..., timeout=timeout) returns a CompletedProcess, detached=True -> subprocess.Popen(...) returned unwaited; runner is injectable so the test drives every path without a live spawn. The helper owns only the three std streams + timeout, never the command shape.

source_artifacts: issue #411: a second spawner (continue-probe/probe.py:_spawn) left stdin inherited, re-introducing the hang done-line 0168 fixed at gate.py's one call site.
